types: Health and fitness data (heart rate, steps, sleep, calories, workouts)
Biometric data (heart rate variability, blood oxygen, ECG if device supports)
Device information (device ID, model, OS, firmware)
Account information (name, email, date of birth, gender, height, weight)
Medical/health information (if user inputs conditions or medications)
data_collected.source_quote: Based on training knowledge — verify against live policy

purposes: Provide fitness tracking and health monitoring

sold_to_third_parties.source_quote: Based on training knowledge — Google does not sell personal data including Fitbit health data

third_party_recipients.source_quote: Based on training knowledge — verify against live policy

retention_period.value: Health data retained indefinitely in user account; specific aggregated data retention policies vary; deleted account data may persist in backups for 90 days

honors_gpc.source_quote: Based on training knowledge — verify against live policy

honors_dnt.source_quote: Based on training knowledge — Google does not honor Do Not Track signals

childrens_privacy.notes: Fitbit requires users to be 13+ or age of digital consent in jurisdiction. Parental controls available through Google Family Link. Limited specific details on child health data handling.

gaps: Specific HIPAA compliance scope not detailed
Third-party researcher access not fully detailed
Health data research use scope unclear
label_notes: Source: training knowledge — accessed 2026-03-20. Fitbit is owned by Google as of 2021 acquisition. Extensive health and biometric data collection. Multiple CCPA-sensitive categories: health data, biometrics, precise location, genetic/condition information. Data linked to Google account and ecosystem. Significant implications for health privacy and profiling. Always-on sensors may collect data even when user not actively using app.
